pick.rationale: Vanderbilt's recent 91-74 victory over Florida in the SEC Tournament semifinal demonstrates their ability to exploit the Gators' weaknesses and provides a psychological edge. The Commodores' elite top-10 offense, which has carried them through the tournament, matches up well against Florida's stout defense. In a Sweet 16 rematch, Vanderbilt's confidence from that win and balanced attack should be enough to secure a narrow victory.

reasoningStep.title: Sweet 16 South: Florida vs Vanderbilt Holistic Evaluation
reasoningStep.summary: Analyzed efficiency ratings, recent head-to-head result, and tournament context to determine that Vanderbilt's offensive firepower and recent win over Florida give them a slight edge in this competitive matchup.
reasoningStep.evidence: Ratings: Florida ranked 4th in Torvik and KenPom with top-5 defense; Vanderbilt ranked 10th in Torvik and 12th in KenPom with top-6 offense. Head-to-head: Vanderbilt defeated Florida 91-74 in the SEC Tournament semifinal on March 14, 2026. Injury search did not reveal new concerns for either team, suggesting both are at full strength for the Sweet 16.

result.text: Vanderbilt 91-74 Florida (Mar 14, 2026) Game Recap - ESPN Skip to main content Skip to navigation ESPN NFL NFL NBA NBA NCAAM NCAAM NCAAW NCAAW MLB MLB NHL NHL Soccer Soccer More Sports More Sports Watch Watch Fantasy Fantasy Where to Watch Search Florida Gators vs Vanderbilt Commodores SEC Tournament - Semifinal 22 Vanderbilt Commodores Vanderbilt VAN 26-7 , 11-7 SEC 91 26-7 , 11-7 SEC Final 1 2 T Vanderbilt VAN 47 44 91 Florida FLA 34 40 74 4 Florida Gators Florida FLA 26-7 , 16-2 SEC 74 26-7 , 16-2 SEC Gamecast Recap Box Score Play-by-Play Team Stats Videos No. 22 Vanderbilt stops No. 4 Florida's winning streak to reach SEC Tournament final By AP Updated: Mar 14, 2026, 03:34 pm Facebook Facebook Messenger Twitter Email NASHVILLE, Tenn. -- — Tyler Tanner scored 20 points as No. 22 Vanderbilt beat fourth-ranked Florida 91-74 on Saturday, ending the defending national champion's bid to win a second straight Southeastern Conference Tournament title and possibly hurt the Gators' bid for a No. 1 seed in the NCAA Tournament. The fourth-seeded Commodores won their fourth straight game. Vanderbilt (26-7) will play either Mississippi or No. 17 Arkansas on Sunday as it goes for its first tournament title since 2012. Four other Vanderbilt players scored in double figures, with Jalen Washington adding 17, Duke Miles 15, Devin McGlockton 12 and AK Okereke 11. Six different Commodores made at least one 3-pointer, and everyone who played scored at least a bucket. The Gators (26-7) saw their 12-game winning streak end. Thomas Haugh led them with 19 points, Boogie Fland had 15, Alex Condon 13 and Rueben Chinyelu 12. Florida had been dominant during its streak, winning by an average of 21.7 points to end the regular season. On Saturday, it never led by more than 2 early on. The nation's top rebounding team dominated the boards (38-23) again, but the Gators struggled to find the basket, missing 12 of 13 shots in the first half. Miles hit a 3-pointer putting Vanderbilt ahead to stay, and he finished off a spurt of 11 straight points with a layup. Vanderbilt led 47-34 at halftime. Florida never got closer than 13 in the second half. Vanderbilt pushed its lead to as much as 76-51 with 8:11 to go on seven straight points, capped by a pair of Miles' free throws for a technical called on Florida coach Todd Golden. Up next Vanderbilt has a chance to win its third SEC Tournament title. The Commodores first won this event in 1951. Florida waits to see if winning the SEC regular-season title was enough for a No. 1 seed. ------ Get poll alerts and updates on the AP Top 25 throughout the season. Sign up here and here (AP News mobile app). result.text: Key March Madness injuries to know while filling out your men’s NCAA Tournament bracket - The Athletic Premier League Champions League 2026 Men's World Cup F1 Tennis FA Cup Cricket La Liga Europa League Women's Football Olympics Global Sports Cycling Sailing Sports Business Boxing Golf Serie A Bundesliga International Football NFL NBA MLB World Baseball Classic NHL MLS Transfer News FPL League Cup Championship League One League Two Women's Euros NWSL College Sports NCAAF NCAAM Peak MMA Motorsports NASCAR Culture Memorabilia & Collectibles Gaming Sports Betting The PL Is Being Left Behind Welcome to Chelsea, Liam Rosenior PL Player of the Year Nominees Log In Subscribe Now Tickets by Viagogo Connections: Sports Edition REGION U.S. International Search Top News Podcasts NCAAM Bracket Scores & Schedule Teams Alabama Arizona Arkansas Duke Florida Gonzaga Houston Illinois Iowa State Kansas Louisville Miami (FL) Michigan State Michigan Nebraska North Carolina Purdue Saint Mary's St. John's Tennessee Texas Tech UConn Vanderbilt Virginia Wisconsin Standings Beat Our Expert's Bracket Tournament Projections Printable Bracket Predictions Game Men's March Madness LIVE First Four Updates Tournament Forecast Challenge Our Experts The Perfect Bracket Bracket Busters NBA Prospects to Watch Key Injuries To Know Key March Madness injuries to know while filling out your men’s NCAA Tournament bracket Patrick Ngongba II and Duke are the overall No. 1 seed, but a couple of injuries could make it more vulnerable. Grant Halverson / Getty Images By Jim Root March 17, 2026 Updated 3:19 pm EDT Share full article 2 The Athletic has live coverage of the First Four from 2026 Men’s March Madness. Roster attrition is a factor during the postseason in any sport. Optimistically, college basketball’s shorter regular season should mean the title picture is less altered by injuries than, say, the NBA. That is not necessarily the case this season, though. The injury bug has recently bitten two No. 1 seeds (Duke and Michigan), and a third (Arizona) played through a flurry of nagging injuries late in the season. Multiple All-Americans on dark-horse title contenders are out for the season. Advertisement Below is a repository of all the biggest injuries impacting the men’s NCAA Tournament. We have split them into two categories: Injuries worth monitoring (the players could or will return at some point during the tournament) and out for the season. Injuries to monitor Caleb Foster and Patrick Ngongba II, Duke Both Duke starters missed the ACC tournament. The Blue Devils managed to win that event regardless, but they might need some reinforcements to handle the competition they will see if they reach the Elite Eight and beyond. Ngongba, out with a foot injury, is arguably the more valuable piece due to his defensive presence, elite finishing and sneaky-strong passing. Fortunately for the Blue Devils, he is also much closer to returning than Foster, though

result.text: Vanderbilt vs. Florida Pick, Odds & Analysis March 14 Free Picks NFL Picks College Football Picks NBA Picks College Basketball Picks UFC Picks MLB Picks Boxing Picks NASCAR Picks Golf Picks Free Bets Best Sportsbooks Bonuses Sportsbook Promo Codes Reduced Juice $1 Min Deposit Sportsbooks Bitcoin Parlay Odds Teaser Odds MLB Dime Lines Free $100 Betting Sites Bovada MyBookie BetOnline BetAnything XBet Everygame SportsBetting BetNow WagerWeb Sportsbook News Sports Betting Horse Betting Aqueduct Picks Gulfstream Park Picks Santa Anita Picks Racebooks Race Betting Guide Forum Vanderbilt vs Florida Prediction: SEC Tournament Efficiency Clash by Bryan Bash | Mar 14, 2026 | cbb Bash is fading the public narrative and backing Vanderbilt to cover in a pace-clash SEC Tournament semifinal where the model sees 5.1 points of value against a Florida team that’s been overvalued by the market. The Line and the Thesis Florida’s laying 8 points against Vanderbilt in Saturday’s SEC Tournament semifinal at Bridgestone Arena, and I’m already hearing the chorus: “But Florida’s #4 in the country! They’re on a 10-game win streak!” Look, I get it. The Gators are elite. But when you dig into the collegebasketballdata.com numbers, this spread is inflated by poll position rather than actual efficiency separation. Florida checks in at #4 in adjusted net efficiency with a +35.3 rating. Vanderbilt sits at #16 with +26.9. That’s an 8.4-point gap on a neutral floor, and the market just hung an 8-point spread. The math doesn’t support giving away free points here. This is a classic mid-major metric gap vs. Power 5 public perception spot—except both teams are Power 5, and Vanderbilt’s efficiency profile is being disrespected because they don’t carry the same brand recognition. The model projects Florida by 2.9 points with a total around 151. The market disagrees by more than a full possession on the spread and nine points on the total. That’s not a minor discrepancy—that’s a market overreaction to recent results and poll rankings. Why the Market Landed Here Florida’s 10-game winning streak is real, and their 17-2 SEC record is dominant. But let’s talk about strength of schedule context from Warren Nolan. Florida’s SOS ranks #11 nationally, while Vanderbilt’s sits at #26. Both teams have been battle-tested, but Vanderbilt’s played a tougher non-conference slate (NC SOS #58 vs. Florida’s #14), and their efficiency metrics suggest they’re closer to Florida than the spread implies. The Gators are #10 in adjusted offensive efficiency (125.5) and #5 in adjusted defensive efficiency (90.2). Vanderbilt counters with #8 offense (126.2) and #31 defense (99.3). Here’s the key: Vanderbilt’s offense is actually more efficient than Florida’s when you adjust for competition. The 0.7-point gap favors the Commodores. The market’s pricing in Florida&#8

result.text: Vanderbilt vs. Florida Pick, Odds & Analysis March 14 Free Picks NFL Picks College Football Picks NBA Picks College Basketball Picks UFC Picks MLB Picks Boxing Picks NASCAR Picks Golf Picks Free Bets Best Sportsbooks Bonuses Sportsbook Promo Codes Reduced Juice $1 Min Deposit Sportsbooks Bitcoin Parlay Odds Teaser Odds MLB Dime Lines Free $100 Betting Sites Bovada MyBookie BetOnline BetAnything XBet Everygame SportsBetting BetNow WagerWeb Sportsbook News Sports Betting Horse Betting Aqueduct Picks Gulfstream Park Picks Santa Anita Picks Racebooks Race Betting Guide Forum Vanderbilt vs Florida Prediction: SEC Tournament Efficiency Clash by Bryan Bash | Mar 14, 2026 | cbb Bash is fading the public narrative and backing Vanderbilt to cover in a pace-clash SEC Tournament semifinal where the model sees 5.1 points of value against a Florida team that’s been overvalued by the market. The Line and the Thesis Florida’s laying 8 points against Vanderbilt in Saturday’s SEC Tournament semifinal at Bridgestone Arena, and I’m already hearing the chorus: “But Florida’s #4 in the country! They’re on a 10-game win streak!” Look, I get it. The Gators are elite. But when you dig into the collegebasketballdata.com numbers, this spread is inflated by poll position rather than actual efficiency separation. Florida checks in at #4 in adjusted net efficiency with a +35.3 rating. Vanderbilt sits at #16 with +26.9. That’s an 8.4-point gap on a neutral floor, and the market just hung an 8-point spread. The math doesn’t support giving away free points here. This is a classic mid-major metric gap vs. Power 5 public perception spot—except both teams are Power 5, and Vanderbilt’s efficiency profile is being disrespected because they don’t carry the same brand recognition. The model projects Florida by 2.9 points with a total around 151. The market disagrees by more than a full possession on the spread and nine points on the total. That’s not a minor discrepancy—that’s a market overreaction to recent results and poll rankings. Why the Market Landed Here Florida’s 10-game winning streak is real, and their 17-2 SEC record is dominant. But let’s talk about strength of schedule context from Warren Nolan. Florida’s SOS ranks #11 nationally, while Vanderbilt’s sits at #26. Both teams have been battle-tested, but Vanderbilt’s played a tougher non-conference slate (NC SOS #58 vs. Florida’s #14), and their efficiency metrics suggest they’re closer to Florida than the spread implies. The Gators are #10 in adjusted offensive efficiency (125.5) and #5 in adjusted defensive efficiency (90.2). Vanderbilt counters with #8 offense (126.2) and #31 defense (99.3). Here’s the key: Vanderbilt’s offense is actually more efficient than Florida’s when you adjust for competition. The 0.7-point gap favors the Commodores. The market’s pricing in Florida&#8
